title: Grand Theft Auto VI set for November 19 launch, Take-Two confirms; $8 billion in bookings eyed
summary: Grand Theft Auto VI video game (GTA 6) will launch on 19 November 2026, announced Take-Two Interactive during its latest earnings report. The company also projected fiscal 2027 bookings of up to $8.2 billion, with analysts expecting the blockbuster game to drive massive sales and industry growth.

title: Malaysia to introduce new rules to protect youth on online platforms
summary: Malaysia is rolling out new regulations from June 1 to shield children from harmful online content. Online platforms must now implement age restrictions for users under 16 and enhance their content moderation policies to ensure a safer digital environment for young Malaysians.

title: Ixigo posts 8.5% rise in Q4 revenue, clocks profit of Rs 32 crore
summary: Ixigo's parent, Le Travenues Technology, reported an 8.5% year-on-year increase in operating revenue to Rs 308 crore for the March quarter, with profit after tax soaring 91% to Rs 32 crore. This growth was driven by a multi-modal strategy and AI-driven cost reductions.

title: Trump calls off AI executive order over concern it could weaken US tech edge
summary: President Trump halted an AI executive order. He feared it would slow America's technological progress. The order aimed to review AI's national security risks. This move comes amid concerns about AI's cybersecurity capabilities. Tech firms were to collaborate voluntarily. The administration seeks to

title: California governor orders a plan to cope with AI job upheaval
summary: California Governor Gavin Newsom on Thursday ordered officials to start work on a plan to mitigate the job-destroying impact of artificial intelligence, the first US state to do so. "California has never sat back and watched as the future happened to us -- and we won't start now," he said.

title: Samsung union to start vote on tentative wage deal
summary: Samsung labour union members will vote on a tentative wage deal this week. This agreement averted a major strike at the South Korean chip giant. The provisional deal was reached following government-mediated talks. The semiconductor division will receive a new bonus pool equivalent to 10.5% of opera

title: SpaceX postpones highly anticipated Starship launch
summary: SpaceX's Starship launch is postponed. A technical glitch delayed the test flight. The company aims for a new attempt on Friday. This mission is crucial for SpaceX's upcoming initial public offering. It also supports NASA's lunar program. The Starship rocket is key for returning humans to the Moon.

title: Swiggy’s shareholder setback; Walmart’s Flipkart push
summary: Happy Friday! Swiggy’s move to become an Indian-owned company hit a setback after the shareholder vote fell short. This and more in today’s ETtech Morning Dispatch.

title: OpenAI held $1 billion revenue lead over Anthropic in Q1: The Information
summary: OpenAI reported $5.7 billion in first-quarter revenue, leading Anthropic by $1 billion. However, Anthropic projects its second-quarter revenue to double to $11 billion with a $600 million profit.

title: Hexaware to buy UK consulting company CPS in Rs 140 crore deal
summary: The Mumbai-headquartered company said the acquisition, to be carried out through its wholly owned subsidiary Hexaware Technologies UK Ltd, is expected to close within two weeks. The deal includes an upfront payment of £6 million, while another £5 million is linked to financial performance targets.
